subject: Mathematics
title: Godel's Incompleteness Theorems
description: A fast breakdown of Godel's Incompleteness Theorems for builders.

hook.text: In 1931, a 25-year-old proved that mathematics can never fully know itself. The news shook the world.

buildup.text: Kurt Godel showed that any consistent system powerful enough to describe arithmetic contains unprovable truths.

discovery.text: His first theorem: there are true statements that the system can never prove. They're invisible to it.

twist.text: His second theorem: no such system can prove its own consistency. Math can never fully trust itself.

climax.text: Hilbert's dream of a complete, consistent foundation for all math was proven impossible.

punchline.text: Some truths are real but forever unprovable. Godel proved it.

quiz.question: What did Godel's first incompleteness theorem prove?
options: Some true statements can never be proven within a consistent system
